name: write_tickets_tool
summary: Mutate the ticket queue: create new tickets, edit fields, add comments, transition status, or change assignee.
Use `transition` to settle the agent's current ticket — moving it to `Done`, `Failed`, or `InReview` is the standard end-of-work move.
constraints: Every action except `create` defaults `key` to the agent's current ticket — omit `key` to act on your own work.
`comment` defaults `author` and `create` defaults `reporter` to the agent's name.
anti_patterns: To look up, list, or search tickets — use `read_tickets_tool`.
When the agent needs read AND write in the same flow — use `manage_tickets_tool` instead of registering both.
To delegate work to another agent — use `assign` with that agent's name; the loop's Path A picks it up next iteration.
cautions: IMPORTANT: status transitions follow a state machine. Allowed: `Todo↔InProgress`, `InProgress↔InReview`, `InProgress`/`InReview` → `Done`/`Failed`. Anything else returns an error — handle the rejection rather than retrying.
IMPORTANT: transitioning to `Todo` clears the assignee. To return to the queue without losing the assignment, prefer `InReview`.
IMPORTANT: `Done` and `Failed` are terminal — no transitions out. Use `InReview` if you want a reviewer to send the ticket back.
ALWAYS settle your current ticket before the response ends. Returning text without `transition` leaves the ticket `InProgress` and the loop will re-pick it next iteration.
output: Plain text confirmation: `Created ticket KEY`, `Edited ticket KEY`, `Commented on KEY`, `Transitioned KEY to STATUS`, or `Assigned KEY to NAME`.
On rejection: an error describing the failed action (missing field, unknown ticket, illegal transition).

key.description: Ticket key (e.g. `TICKET-3`). Required for `edit`, `comment`, `transition`, `assign`; ignored by `create`. Defaults to the agent's current ticket.

summary.description: For `create` (required) or `edit` (optional): the one-line title. Empty string is rejected.

description.description: For `create` (optional, defaults empty) or `edit` (optional): the long-form body.

type.description: For `create`: ticket type (e.g. `task`, `bug`, `research`). Defaults to `task`. Determines which agents can pick it up via Path B.

reporter.description: For `create`: who filed the ticket. Defaults to the agent's name. Set this when creating tickets on behalf of another agent or a human.

author.description: For `comment`: who wrote the comment. Defaults to the agent's name.

status.description: For `transition`: target status. One of `Todo`, `InProgress`, `InReview`, `Done`, `Failed`. Must be a legal transition from the current status.

assignee.description: For `assign`: agent or human name to take ownership. Empty string clears the assignee. Setting this to another agent's name is how you delegate work — that agent's loop picks it up next iteration.
